Sacnmento. Glilornir 95815 Trlcphone (916) 923.23i5 (916) 923.1825
0ctober 1, 1990
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Comrnission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re: Request for Informal Advice
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
This letter is written to request informal advice from the Fair
Political Practices Cormission pursuant to Government Code Section
83114(b) and 2 Cal. Code of Regs. 18329 on behalf of the California
Pol ice Chiefs' Association.
The question posed to the Cormission is as follows: Under the
provisions of the Political Reform Act, may a local police department
enter into contracts for required police training services with
Company X where Company X has been a source of $250 or more income
to the Chief of Police instructing a State certified and reimbursed
training course within the last 12 months?
More specifically, for the purpose of this request, the facts are
as follows: Company X is a certified provider for continuing education
and required police training, authorized and reimbursed by the
Department of Justice Commission on Peace 0fficer Standards and
Training (P.0.S,T.). The Police Chief, a court qualified training
expert and one of two instructors certified by the state to teach
this specific P.0.S.T. reimbursed course, was employed by Company
X within the last 12 months to teach the course at a P.0.S.T.
determined rate of pay. Company X is certified by P.O.S.T. to
exclusively provide this course, which is taught only by the Chief
and the other instructor. It should be noted the Chief is not
directly involved in the selection or assignment of training courses
for his department. This is delegated through the chain of command
to the Trai ni ng Unit.
Company X has also contracted with the Police Department in the
past to provide training and promotional testing prior to the current
Ctrief 's administration. The scope of Company X's involvement with
the City in providing contemplated future courses could include
both P.0.S.T. certified/reimbursable courses as wel I as training
not to be reimbursed to the department by the state. Under no
circumstances would the Chief be employed for any non-certified/
reimbursed training nor would the Chief be involved in any decision
making effecting CompanY X'
EXECUIIVE DIFECTOB


Page 2
Many P.0.S.T. courses require the exclusive expertise of police
chiefs, which is not commonly available in the private sector.
It should be noted that there is past practice in this matter state-
wide.
Your prompt attention in rendering an opinion wou.ld be most
appreciated as Association members comprise a significant number
of the instructors for P.0,S.T. approved peace officer training
courses. The Association objective is to properly advise its members
as to this matter so as to avoid potential confl icts of interest.
Please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned if you have any
questi ons about this request.
Larry Todd , C ha i rman
C. P. C. A. Standards and Ethi cs Cornmi ttee
